exhaustionMode.name: When Is Exhaustion Applied?
exhaustionMode.hint: Controls when the exhaustion trigger fires. 'After Combat' queues exhaustion until combat ends; all other modes apply it immediately.

exhaustOnFirstDeathFail.name: Trigger on First Death Save Failure
exhaustOnFirstDeathFail.hint: When enabled, exhaustion triggers on the first failed death save instead of when the character gets back up.

saveMode.name: Require a Save to Avoid Exhaustion
saveMode.hint: Roll to avoid exhaustion when the trigger fires. CON Save uses the character's Constitution modifier; Flat d20 is an unmodified die roll.

baseSaveDC.name: Base Save DC
baseSaveDC.hint: Base DC for the save roll. In After Combat mode, increases by 1 per down (e.g. 3 downs = DC 13 at base 10). Set to 0 to disable combat exhaustion triggers.

afterCombatCheckMode.name: After Combat: Exhaustion Mode
afterCombatCheckMode.hint: Only applies when 'After Combat' is selected above. Choose whether exhaustion is applied without a roll, with a save for a single level, or with a save for all stacked levels.

enableLongRestQuality.name: Enable Long Rest Quality
enableLongRestQuality.hint: When enabled, the GM is prompted to choose a rest quality tier at the start of each long rest, determining how much exhaustion is recovered. Requires a reload.

exhaustionOverrideSwap.name: Swap Exhaustion Ruleset
exhaustionOverrideSwap.hint: Flips exhaustion to the opposite ruleset. 2014 rules → applies 2024 mechanics. 2024 rules → applies 2014 mechanics. Requires a reload.

standard.label: Standard Rest
standard.description: Rough sleeping, camping, or a basic tavern.

quality.label: Comfortable Rest
quality.description: A private room at a good inn or similarly comfortable lodging.

luxury.label: Luxury Rest
luxury.description: A luxury suite at a fine inn, bathhouse, or accommodations with exceptional comfort and care.
